title: senecio tournefortii subsp. cespitosus
intro: Senecio tournefortii subsp. cespitosus, also known as a subspecies of the Tournefort's groundsel, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is commonly found in dry to mesic areas. The medicinal properties of Senecio tournefortii subsp. cespitosus lie in its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and astringent, making it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also known to have health benefits such as lowering blood pressure and reducing fever, making it a valuable component in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, Senecio tournefortii subsp. cespitosus can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various ailments, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
